title: All Things Decay And Die
content:
All things decay with time: The forest sees
The growth and down-fall of her aged trees;
That timber tall, which three-score lustres stood
The proud dictator of the state-like wood,
I mean the sovereign of all plants, the oak,
Droops, dies, and falls without the cleaver's stroke.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "All Things Decay And Die"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
